items.title: PPI +6.0% Y/Y — Truck Transport +8.1%, Services +5.5%: Not Just a Gasoline Story
items.idea: April PPI +1.4% m/m headline (3x the +0.5% estimate) and +1.0% m/m core (vs +0.3% forecast). Year-over-year: 6.0% headline and 5.2% core — highest since 2022. Gasoline drove goods prices but ex-food/energy still +0.7% m/m and +4.6% y/y. Services +1.2% m/m and +5.5% y/y. Machinery and equipment wholesaling margins spiked +3.5%. Truck transportation +8.1% in April alone — Boockvar had flagged this from trucking company contacts.
items.angle: This is broad-based — the Hormuz shock is now embedding itself in wholesale prices across every channel. CPI is the lagging indicator. By the time core CPI fully reflects this, the damage is already priced in.
items.tweetA: PPI just printed +6.0% y/y. Core ex-food/energy +4.6%. Truck transport alone +8.1% in April. This is not a gasoline story — it's a broad-based repricing. The question is not if the Fed hikes. It's whether Warsh hikes before the market breaks.
items.tweetB: Services PPI +5.5% y/y. Machinery wholesaling margins +3.5% in one month. The Hormuz shock is now embedding in wholesale prices across every channel. CPI is a lagging indicator. By the time it shows up in core CPI, the damage is already done. Budget for higher-for-longer on every timeline.

items.title: IEA: 12.8M b/d Gone Since War Began — Aramco CEO: 100M Barrels Lost Per Week
items.idea: IEA reports global oil supply fell 12.8 million barrels per day since the Iran war began, with inventories depleting at record pace. Saudi Aramco CEO: markets are losing 100 million barrels every week the strait remains shut, with stockpiles running dangerously low. Morgan Stanley warns oil market in a 'race against time' with Brent potentially hitting $150/bbl by summer if closure persists into June. IEA warns that even immediate reopening means supply falls average 3.9 million b/d for the rest of 2026. Iran retained 70% of pre-war missile capabilities.
items.angle: The market is pricing a near-term deal. The IEA is telling you the physical damage is already done — storage depletion is at record pace regardless of when Hormuz reopens. Every day of delay is 100M barrels gone.
items.tweetA: IEA: global oil supply down 12.8 million b/d since the Iran war started. Aramco CEO: 100M barrels lost every week the strait stays closed. Morgan Stanley: Brent $150 by summer. Oil is trading at $98. Do the math on what happens if the Muscat diplomacy fails.
items.tweetB: IEA warning: even if Hormuz reopens tomorrow, supply falls 3.9 million b/d average for the rest of 2026. Storage depletion already happened. Iran kept 70% of its missiles. UK is sending warships. The market bounces on every hint of a deal. Each bounce is a gift to build energy exposure.

items.title: PPI Multi-Source View: 10-yr at 4.49%, BoJ Hike Next Month, ECB Stagflation Warning
items.idea: US wholesale prices surged 6.0% y/y in April (highest since 2022), core PPI 5.2% vs 4.3% forecast. The 10-year Treasury yield spiked to 4.49%, highest since July. BoJ signaling rate hike as early as next month on upside inflation risks. ECB's Rehn warns of 'first signs of a stagflationary shock.' Japan 20-year yield 3.495% — highest since 1997. Japanese funds dumping most US debt since 2022. Central banks from Tokyo to Frankfurt shifting from cuts to potential hikes.
items.angle: This is a global synchronized policy tightening event driven by a single supply shock. The dollar benefits short-term but every central bank that tightens further to fight imported inflation is doing it while their growth is collapsing.
items.tweetA: Month 3 of Hormuz closure. BoJ signaling a rate hike. ECB warning of stagflationary shock. Japan 20-yr yields at 1997 highs. Everyone is reacting to the same event — and none of the central banks can solve it with monetary policy. The wrong tool for the wrong problem.
items.tweetB: Japan 20-yr yield hit 3.495%, highest since 1997. Japanese funds dumping most US debt since 2022. BoJ is about to hike. Japan is the largest holder of US Treasuries. When domestic yields become attractive enough, that capital comes home. The UST market is not pricing this repatriation risk.

items.title: CPI 3.8% — Real Wages -0.3% Y/Y, Gasoline +28.4%, Stagflation Is No Longer a Risk
items.idea: April CPI headline +0.6% m/m, 3.8% y/y — highest since May 2023. Core CPI +0.4% m/m, 2.8% y/y — above 2.7% consensus. Energy +17.9% y/y: gasoline +28.4%, fuel oil +54.3%. Real average hourly wages: -0.5% m/m and -0.3% y/y. Workers losing ground in real terms. Classic stagflation: high inflation, falling real income, a central bank that cannot cut.
items.angle: Stagflation was previously a risk scenario. Real wages going negative y/y while CPI runs at 3.8% is not a risk — it is the current state. The consumption data hasn't broken yet, but the income math is negative.
items.tweetA: CPI 3.8%. Real wages: -0.3% y/y. Fuel oil +54.3% annually. This is stagflation — high inflation, falling real income, a central bank that cannot cut. Kevin Warsh inherits this on Friday. Rate hike odds went from 1% to 30% in one month. The policy error is already baked in.
items.tweetB: Markets closed green after 3.8% CPI. The assumption is this is just Iran. But CPI was running hot before Hormuz closed. Iran is the accelerant on a fire that was already burning. That is a different thesis than transitory war inflation — and it changes the Fed calculus completely.

items.title: Kevin Warsh Takes Fed on Friday — Rate Hike Probability Jumps to 30% From 1%
items.idea: Warsh was confirmed 51-45 (Fetterman sole Democratic crossover vote) and takes over from Powell on Friday. He inherits: 3.8% CPI, PPI at 6% y/y, oil above $100, stagflation concerns. Odds of a 2026 rate hike jumped from 1% to 30% after combined CPI/PPI prints. Warsh is known as hawkish and critical of asset price inflation. Takes over mid-crisis with every lever compromised.
items.angle: This is a genuine leadership transition at the worst possible moment. Powell had forward guidance credibility built up over years. Warsh starts from zero with the hottest inflation since 2022 and bond markets already pricing hike risk.
items.tweetA: Kevin Warsh takes the Fed on Friday. He inherits: 3.8% CPI, PPI at 6%, oil at $98, 30% hike probability. Powell waited too long in 2022 and got lucky. Warsh gets no such luck — he is taking the wheel mid-crisis with every lever compromised.
items.tweetB: 30% probability of a rate hike in 2026 — one month ago it was 1%. 10-yr at 4.49%. 30-yr near 19-year high. Equities still at ATH. One of these markets is pricing the wrong scenario. History says it is not the bond market.

items.source: Neil Sethi / As We Approach the Open
items.title: Trump First Beijing Visit in 9 Years — Jensen Huang Boards AF1 as Last-Minute Addition
items.idea: Trump arrived in Beijing for the first US state visit in 9 years. Brings Musk, Tim Cook, Jensen Huang (last-minute addition), Kelly Ortberg, Larry Fink. Meets Xi at Great Hall of the People tomorrow in a 36-hour summit. Key agenda: Iran war (China's revenue to Iran), Taiwan arms sale (Trump delayed $14B package), trade truce extension. Jensen Huang boarding AF1 confirmed AI/chips are at the center of talks.
items.angle: This is the most consequential US-China meeting in a decade, happening at the convergence of an Iran war, a chip cold war, and a potential Taiwan crisis. Jensen Huang's addition was unscripted — which means the chip discussion was not planned by either side's diplomats.
items.tweetA: First US state visit to China in 9 years. Jensen Huang joined Air Force One as a last-minute addition. The AI and chip war was always going to be on the table — now it is officially on the plane. Trump wants trade deals; Xi wants Taiwan commitments. Neither gets what they want in 36 hours.
items.tweetB: Trump delayed a $14B Taiwan weapons package to smooth the Beijing summit. He brought Musk, Jensen Huang, Tim Cook, Larry Fink. This trip is not about soybeans — it is about who controls the AI supply chain for the next decade. The most important meeting of 2026 is happening right now.

items.title: JGB 29-Year High at 2.59% — Japan Repatriation Risk for $1T+ in US Treasuries
items.idea: 10-yr JGB at 2.59%, a 29-year high. Japan 20-yr yield at 3.495%, highest since 1997. Japan is the largest single holder of US Treasuries. Foreigners own about 30% of US Treasuries, 77% of German bunds, about 50% of French OATs. Boockvar: 'There is a JGB yield level where that money is going to come back home.' Japanese funds have already dumped the most US debt since 2022 this month.
items.angle: This is not being priced in equities or risk assets. Japan holds more US Treasuries than any other nation. If the JGB yield differential closes enough to make domestic bonds attractive, the forced repatriation is a structural UST demand shock.
items.tweetA: 10-yr JGB at 2.59%, 29-year high. Japan 20-yr at 1997 highs. Japan is the largest holder of US Treasuries. There is a JGB yield level at which that money comes home. We may be approaching it. $1T+ in USTs potentially in play. Not priced in.
items.tweetB: Boockvar's insight: foreigners own 77% of German bunds and are selling to manage their own energy price shock. The global bond sell-off is not just about US inflation — it is repatriation flows from countries that need cash to buy oil at $98. The feedback loop runs from Hormuz to JGBs to USTs.

items.source: Neil Sethi / As We Approach the Open
items.title: Samsung Strike: April 23 Rally Caused 58% Foundry Drop in a Single Day
items.idea: 41,000 Samsung workers threatening 18-day strike. The April 23 one-day rally already caused 58% drop in foundry production and 18% fall in memory production for that single day. Samsung lost $66B in market cap intraday before recovering. Union demands: 15% of operating profit as performance bonuses. South Korean cabinet holding emergency meeting.
items.angle: A 58% foundry production drop from one day's strike action is the single most important data point for the global memory and advanced packaging market right now. If this strike proceeds for 18 days, the memory supply shock arrives on top of an already tight market.
items.tweetA: Samsung workers threatened an 18-day strike. The April 23 rally alone caused 58% foundry production drop and 18% memory fall in one day. If 41,000 workers walk out for 18 days, the global memory market gets tight fast. We just priced $NVDA to ATH — memory is the next bottleneck to watch.
items.tweetB: Samsung lost $66B in market cap intraday on strike news. The real risk is not one strike day — it is precedent. Korean labor is watching what Samsung workers can extract from the world's largest chipmaker. Wage inflation and supply chain risk are the same trade here.

items.title: Copper All-Time High at $14,000/Ton — +75% Since October 2023
items.idea: Copper hit a new all-time high today at $14,000 per metric ton, up +75% since October 2023. Multiple demand drivers converging: AI buildout (data centers require enormous copper), energy transition (EVs, grid upgrades), and Iran war risk premium on commodity supply chains. US 30-yr yield nearing 19-year high simultaneously.
items.angle: Copper at ATH while equities are ATH and bonds are selling off is an inflationary boom signal. Copper has been the most reliable leading indicator for global industrial demand for decades.
items.tweetA: Copper hit $14,000/ton, +75% since October 2023. Nobody is building new copper mines fast enough. The AI buildout, the energy transition, and the Iran war premium are all converging on the same red metal. $FCX $SCCO. The commodity cycle is not over.
items.tweetB: Copper at ATH, US 30-yr near 19-year high, CPI at 3.8%, equities at ATH. One of these is sending a signal the others are ignoring. Commodities have been right about inflation longer than bonds have. I keep coming back to this chart.

charts.desc: Copper chart +75% from Oct 2023 — CHART WORTH SAVING

items.source: Neil Sethi / As We Approach the Open
items.title: India Raises Gold Import Duty to 15% From 6% — Modi Asked Citizens to Stop Buying
items.idea: India raised gold import duties from 6% to 15% (10% basic + 5% tax). PM Modi asked citizens to curb bullion purchases for a year. India's average monthly gold import rose to 83 tonnes in Jan-Feb 2026 (from 53 tonnes average in 2025). India gold demand nearly doubled y/y in Q1 2026 to a record $25B. S&P Global: energy costs remain primary rupee pressure.
items.angle: India is the world's second-largest gold consumer. When they raise import duties this aggressively, it is a signal that dollar demand is overwhelming their currency defense mechanism. The demand itself — doubling y/y — tells you how the world's largest middle class is thinking about currency debasement.
items.tweetA: India raised gold import duty from 6% to 15%. PM Modi asked citizens to stop buying gold for a year. India's gold demand doubled y/y in Q1 to a record $25B. When the world's second-largest consumer starts restricting imports — that is a demand signal, not a supply problem.
items.tweetB: Modi taxed gold imports at 15% because Indian gold demand doubled and it is crushing the rupee. Energy costs are the primary pressure per S&P Global. India chose protectionism over devaluation. That tells you everything about what they think comes next for the dollar.

items.title: Google Discloses First AI-Generated Zero-Day Exploit Used in Live Attack
items.idea: Google's Threat Intelligence Group disclosed the first known instance of a threat actor using an AI-generated zero-day exploit in a live attack. This comes the same week Ethereum's Clear Signing standard (ERC-7730) launches to combat blind-signing exploits that enabled $1.5B+ in crypto theft. AI is simultaneously the offense and the defense in the new security paradigm.
items.angle: This is the crossing of a threshold. Every security vendor has been saying AI-enabled attacks were coming. Now there is a confirmed case. Offense typically moves faster than defense in the first phase of any new attack vector.
items.tweetA: Google confirmed: a threat actor used an AI-generated zero-day exploit in a live attack. First known case. It will not be the last. The time between AI capability and AI weaponization is measured in months, not years. $PANW $CRWD $S are not optional infrastructure.
items.tweetB: The first AI-generated zero-day in a live attack, the same week Ethereum launches Clear Signing to fight blind-signing exploits. AI is simultaneously the offense and the defense. The old security perimeter model is dead. The new one is about AI-speed response to AI-speed attack.

items.source: Neil Sethi / As We Approach the Open
items.title: ECB 'First Signs of Stagflationary Shock' — France Unemployment 5-Year High at 8.1%
items.idea: France unemployment hit 8.1% in Q1, a 5-year high (expected 7.8%). Bank of France Chief Economist Debrun: 'Today we are seeing the first signs of the shock.' Eurozone Q1 GDP barely +0.1% q/q. ECB's Rehn: 'first signs of a stagflationary shock, with Q1 euro-area growth barely positive and inflation accelerating to 3%.' Industry and construction activity declining in May.
items.angle: Europe is the canary in the coal mine. Closest to the supply shock geographically. The ECB's stagflation language is where the Fed will be in 3 months if Hormuz stays closed.
items.tweetA: France unemployment just hit 8.1%, a 5-year high. Bank of France: first signs of the shock. ECB using the word stagflationary. Eurozone Q1 GDP barely +0.1% q/q. Europe is getting hit first because it is closest to the supply shock. The US follows with a lag.
items.tweetB: The ECB is trapped. Cut and you fuel stagflation. Hike and you break the economy. This was Germany's nightmare scenario: no energy sovereignty, dependent on the Middle East, unable to use monetary policy as a fix. The ECB calling this stagflationary is not a forecast. It is an admission.

items.title: The Fragile Mix: Buy-the-Dip + Choke-Point Trades Running Simultaneously
items.idea: Rosenberg's framework: markets running two instincts in parallel — buy every dip on the assumption that tough talk doesn't translate into catastrophe, and chase crowded trades around global choke points. He calls this a 'fragile mix as economic pains mount.' Last year's industrial metals episode: speculative hoarding triggered a crowded trade that eventually corrected. Hormuz closed; Taiwan open — 'for how long?'
items.angle: The insight is that the dip-buyers and the choke-point traders have converged into the same trade. If either assumption breaks — whether the Iran deal falls apart or the 'nothing bad actually happens' thesis cracks — both trades unwind simultaneously.
items.tweetA: Rosenberg's framework: markets are running two trades in parallel — buy every dip and chase every choke point. He calls this a fragile mix. Last time these trades converged in industrial metals, they corrected 30%+. The dip-buyers and the macro traders are now the same position.
items.tweetB: Hormuz is closed. Taiwan is open. Rosenberg asks: for how long? Two choke points, two asset classes at ATH. The market is pricing the continuation of both current states simultaneously. That is a very specific and fragile bet to be making into a Fed chair transition.

items.title: Fade the Wall When They Jack Targets and Push Out Cuts — DipOrTunity or Real Break?
items.idea: Bond Beat's contrarian framework: as Global Wall jacks equity targets and pushes rate cuts further out, it becomes easier to fade. Inflation above the Fed Funds Rate. Negative real earnings but spending robust. Hot CPI sparked a rates sell-off. The key question: is this a DipOrTunity ahead of the 30-yr auction, or a real break in the bond market?
items.angle: The observation that it is 'easier to fade' when consensus is most bullish on equity is grounded in the data. The 30-yr auction today is the immediate catalyst test for whether bond markets have already priced in Warsh.
items.tweetA: Bond Beat makes the contrarian call: as Global Wall jacks equity targets and pushes out rate cuts, it becomes easier to fade. Inflation above the Fed Funds Rate. Negative real earnings but spending holds. 30-yr auction today. Is this a dip to buy or the beginning of a real bond market break?
items.tweetB: Inflation is above the Fed Funds Rate right now. Real earnings are negative. Yet spending is holding. The Fed is behind the curve heading into a chair transition. The bond market is telling you something the equity market is not priced to hear.

items.title: When SaaS Goes Headless: The 5 New Moat Criteria for the Agentic Era
items.idea: Salesforce launched a 'headless' product — APIs that largely already existed. a16z's real insight: agents bypass UIs, so the old moat (human muscle memory + interface stickiness) is dead. New defensibility hierarchy: (1) compliance-critical data and audit trails; (2) operational logic that agents need to act safely; (3) multi-party network effects where product becomes coordination infrastructure; (4) real-world execution layer (field workers, logistics, payments); (5) proprietary data exhaust generated by being inside the execution loop. AI lowers cost of recreating the first 80% of any system of record.
items.angle: The most important line: 'AI lowers the cost of recreating the first 80% of a SoR. The remaining 20% — exceptions, approvals, compliance, edge-case workflows — is what separates a useful wedge from a true replacement.' This is the SaaS moat calculus investors need now.
items.tweetA: Salesforce goes headless and calls it a product launch. a16z's actual insight: the UI moat is dead — agents don't need browsers. But the REAL moat is the compliance-critical trust layer. ERP replacements require your auditors and regulators as direct stakeholders. That doesn't export cleanly. $CRM $SAP
items.tweetB: New SaaS defensibility in the agentic era: UI moat dead. What survives: compliance-critical audit trails, workflow logic agents need to act safely, multi-party network effects, real-world execution. The next durable system of record will not just store records — it will dispatch people.

items.title: Nvidia China Share 90% to 50% — But Sanctions Created the Domestic Market They Needed
items.idea: Nvidia's CEO confirmed China GPU market share fell from 90% to 50% since the US blocked essentially all GPU shipments. D2D's read: reality is much more complicated. Microsoft just certified first PRC GPUs for Windows compliance (desktop parts, but symbolic). PRC optics and glass substrates: D2D notes PRC companies 'could leapfrog US companies' in this area. D2D's honest assessment: 'Are they working? Sortof. Maybe. Possibly not.'
items.angle: The sanctions definitely reduced Nvidia's revenue but also created a captive domestic market and concentrated state-backed investment into PRC semis at a level that would not have happened otherwise. You may have built the competitor you were trying to prevent.
items.tweetA: Nvidia's China GPU share fell from 90% to 50% since the export ban. The official PRC line: incredible boon to domestic competitors. D2D's actual read: the reality is much more complicated. Microsoft just certified the first PRC GPUs. They are desktop chips today. What are they in 3 years?
items.tweetB: The GPU sanctions did not stop PRC AI development — they concentrated it. State-backed capital redirected to domestic vendors. VC funding returned after years of pause. PRC semis is the strongest it has ever been. The sanctions worked short-term and are building the competition long-term. Both things are true.

items.title: PRC Fabs 30% Domestic Tools (From Low Teens) — Shenghe Jingwei $36B IPO +70%
items.idea: PRC fabs now purchase 30% of tools from PRC suppliers, up from low teens a year ago. Biggest PRC IPO of 2026: Shenghe Jingwei (semiconductor packaging), $36B market cap, +70% since April listing — essentially SMIC's packaging line spun off, with SMIC on US entity list. Best-performing PRC IPO: Lianxun Instrument (semis test/measurement). Yixing Intelligence raised RMB 1.5B for a RISC-V AI accelerator. VC funding resuming after years of pause.
items.angle: The semiconductor ecosystem scorecard: export controls have not stopped PRC semis — they created a wave of domestic vendors now hitting IPO scale. Shenghe Jingwei at $36B from an April listing is the size of a mid-cap US industrial company.
items.tweetA: Biggest PRC IPO of 2026: Shenghe Jingwei (semiconductor packaging), $36B market cap, +70% since April. Lianxun Instrument (semis test equipment) is the best-performing IPO of the year. PRC fabs went from low-teen to 30% domestic tool purchasing in 12 months. The export controls didn't stop the buildout — they redirected the capex.
items.tweetB: Cornerstone Capital (基石资本) — the VC firm you've never heard of — holds stakes in Changxin Memory, YMTC, Biren, and most high-flying PRC semis companies. When PRC semis VC froze and then thawed, these were the positions that moved first. Understanding who owns this ecosystem matters.

items.title: Clarity Act 309 Pages — 100+ Amendments, Ethics Provisions Missing, Citi $143k Target at Stake
items.idea: Senate Banking Committee unveiled 309-page Clarity Act. Core: SEC/CFTC split with most digital assets as commodities; DeFi developer protections; stablecoin yield ban on passive holdings. Missing: ethics provisions re: Trump family crypto dealings. Many Democrats say dead on arrival without them. All 13 Republican votes needed; Sen. Kennedy publicly uncommitted. SEIU, AFT, NEA, AFSCME sent joint letter opposing, citing pension fund risk. Citi has tied $143k BTC target to passage, projecting $15B additional ETF inflows.
items.angle: The market is priced for passage. Citi explicitly models $15B in ETF inflows as conditional on this bill. If it fails — non-trivial with 100+ amendments and missing ethics provisions — the BTC ceiling gets revised down materially.
items.tweetA: Clarity Act drops 309 pages at midnight. SEC/CFTC split, DeFi dev protections, stablecoin yield ban. Missing: ethics provisions for Trump family crypto. Dems say dead on arrival without it. Citi tied their $143k BTC target directly to this passing. All Republican votes needed. Thursday markup.
items.tweetB: 100+ amendments to the Clarity Act before the markup vote. SEIU, AFT, NEA sent a joint letter opposing it over pension fund risk. Every Republican vote needed and Kennedy is uncommitted. Crypto legislation in the US is a lot harder than the BTC price suggests. The market is priced for passage. The bill is not.

items.title: JPM, BlackRock, Morgan Stanley All Racing to Be Treasury Layer for $320B Stablecoin Market
items.idea: JPMorgan filed JLTXX — its 2nd tokenized MMF on Ethereum, structured for stablecoin reserve requirements under GENIUS Act (invests in Treasuries, cash, overnight repo). BlackRock filed a Stablecoin Reserve Vehicle the same week. Morgan Stanley's MSNXX launched last month targeting the same market. Three largest financial institutions racing to become yield infrastructure for a $320B stablecoin market growing 28% annually. RWA market grew 200% y/y to $30B. JLTXX sits on top of public Ethereum as its base layer.
items.angle: JPMorgan is deploying permissioned institutional finance on public Ethereum. The three largest banks are competing to be the yield layer underneath stablecoins. The financialization of crypto infrastructure is happening from the top down.
items.tweetA: JPMorgan, BlackRock, and Morgan Stanley all filed tokenized Treasury products in the same week — all targeting stablecoin reserve requirements. Three largest banks racing to become yield infrastructure for a $320B market growing 28% annually. RWA market +200% y/y to $30B. The banks figured out crypto before crypto figured out the banks.
items.tweetB: JPMorgan JLTXX invests in Treasuries and repo — sitting on top of PUBLIC Ethereum as its base layer. Read that again. JPMorgan is deploying permissioned institutional finance on a public blockchain. That is not a pilot. That is production. The $ETH infrastructure thesis is playing out at the highest level.

items.title: Clear Signing ERC-7730 Launches — End to Blind Signing Behind $1.5B+ in Crypto Theft
items.idea: Ethereum working group launched Clear Signing (ERC-7730): human-readable transaction descriptors, auditor attestations, cryptographic fingerprints. Blind signing was the attack vector behind: $1.5B Bybit exploit, $235M WazirX breach, CoW DAO domain hijacking. Working group includes Ledger, Trezor, MetaMask, WalletConnect, Fireblocks.
items.angle: This removes a reliably exploited attack class responsible for $2B+ in losses. The counterpoint: adoption requires wallets to implement and users to upgrade — slow historically.
items.tweetA: The $1.5B Bybit hack, the $235M WazirX breach, the CoW DAO hijacking — all the same playbook: get a signer to approve something they couldn't understand. ERC-7730 Clear Signing fixes this at the wallet layer. Ledger, Trezor, MetaMask, Fireblocks all in. Most important Ethereum security upgrade in years.
items.tweetB: Blind signing is the buffer overflow of crypto — a fundamental flaw exploited for years and everyone knew about it. Clear Signing finally ships — the same week Google confirms AI-generated zero-days in live attacks. The attack surface is expanding faster than the defenses. ERC-7730 is necessary but not sufficient.

items.title: Decoupling Is Real — But Intermediate Goods Are the Hidden China Channel Through Vietnam
items.idea: China's share of US imports has plummeted (gross trade flows). BUT Hsu/Peng/Wu (2024) found 'substantial increase in indirect dependence on China via suppliers in Vietnam and Mexico.' Transshipment is minor (at most 18% per DiPippo). Most PCs now made in Vietnam (were China 2 years ago). FDI into China collapsed. New arrangement: China sells components, third countries do assembly. Noah's warning: 'It would be a shame if Trump throws this all away in exchange for a few soybean purchases.'
items.angle: The decoupling headline says we are winning; the intermediate goods data says the dependence is more structurally embedded than the gross import numbers show. The summit happening today directly threatens a decade of bipartisan work.
items.tweetA: The decoupling is real — but the mechanism is more subtle. China lost final assembly to Vietnam. But China now supplies the components to those assemblers. Hsu/Peng/Wu: substantial increase in indirect China dependence via Vietnam suppliers. The headline says decoupled. The supply chain says differently.
items.tweetB: Noah's warning: Trump might throw away a decade of bipartisan decoupling progress in exchange for a few soybean purchases. China's playbook is clear — give Trump a photo op, extract Taiwan commitments, survive the trip. US supply chains still run through Chinese components. This summit matters.

items.title: Bitcoin Institutional Floor Holding — ETFs + Corporate Treasuries Both Supporting Simultaneously
items.idea: Bitcoin refusing to break down despite 3.8% CPI, hot PPI, and Hormuz closure. Ecoinometrics: demand improving from two institutional sources simultaneously — ETFs and public companies (corporate treasuries). Bitcoin has not broken cleanly above its 200-day MA but is not rolling over. Bitcoin ETFs saw $233M net outflows Tuesday but BTC bounced from $80k to $80.7k — the floor is holding.
items.angle: The interesting signal is that BTC held the floor while ETF flows actually went negative Tuesday. The demand floor is coming from corporate treasuries filling the gap. That is a structural change in the buyer base.
items.tweetA: Bitcoin refusing to break down despite 3.8% CPI, hot PPI, and Hormuz closure. Ecoinometrics data: ETF AND corporate treasury demand both recovering simultaneously. The institutional floor is holding. Two pillars at the same time — ETFs had $233M in outflows Tuesday and BTC still bounced.
items.tweetB: BTC at $80.7k, 30-day realized vol at yearly low, refusing to break below $80k. The market says inflation doesn't matter for Bitcoin. But that's only true if the institutional floor holds. If ETF outflows and corporate treasury selling coincide — who is the marginal buyer? That is the question to watch.

items.title: Optimism OP Enterprise — Institutional Chains + Buyback Tied to Sequencer Revenue First Time
items.idea: OP Labs launched OP Enterprise: managed infrastructure for institutional chains without in-house ops. Bitpanda Vision Chain = first Fully Managed deployment. ether.fi migrated $220M TVL to OP Mainnet with zero downtime. Privacy Boost: confidential transaction infrastructure for regulated institutions. New buyback: OP token demand tied directly to sequencer revenue for the first time. Enters Q2 with pipeline of institutional revenue streams independent of speculative trading.
items.angle: For the first time, OP token holders have a direct claim on cash flows rather than just narrative. Sequencer revenue scaling with institutional volume is a real earnings story.
items.tweetA: Optimism launched OP Enterprise — institutional blockchains without in-house ops. ether.fi migrated $220M TVL with zero downtime. OP token buybacks are now tied directly to sequencer revenue for the first time. If institutional volume scales, this is a real earnings story for OP holders — not a narrative bet.
items.tweetB: Privacy Boost on Optimism brings confidential transactions for regulated institutions keeping payroll and treasury off public chains. That addressable market has been ignored by the entire L1/L2 ecosystem. If this works, every Fortune 500 treasury function is a potential L2 sequencer fee.

items.title: NUPL-MVRV Harmonic Composite — Structural Bear Market Signal; Only 4 Times in 14 Years
items.idea: The NUPL-MVRV Harmonic Composite combines the two most well-known Bitcoin market cycle indicators using harmonic mean, so neither dominates. Key signal: when both NUPL and MVRV simultaneously move into negative territory, it has occurred only 4 times in 14 years. Historically marks structural bear market ends. Current reading: not there yet, but this is the signal worth monitoring.
items.angle: This is a structural state indicator, not a timing tool. Four occurrences in 14 years means it is extremely rare and meaningful. The question is whether institutional participation changes the on-chain behavior of realized cap in ways that alter historical thresholds.
items.tweetA: NUPL-MVRV Harmonic Composite: combining the two most trusted on-chain cycle indicators via harmonic mean so neither dominates. When both go negative simultaneously — only 4 times in 14 years. Each time marked a structural bear market end. Not there yet. But this is the one signal worth watching.
items.tweetB: The NUPL-MVRV composite does not tell you when the bottom is — it tells you IF you are in the kind of environment where bottoms historically form. Four occurrences in 14 years. The question is whether institutional participation has shifted the thresholds. Worth tracking closely.

items.title: Anthropic $44B ARR — 'Differentiation Is a Moral Obligation' (Shannon Agrees)
items.idea: Anthropic crossed a $44B run rate. OpenAI deployed $4B fund. Sierra raised at $15B. Framework: Claude Shannon proved mathematically that information IS surprise. A predictable message contains zero information. A universe of identical people = zero information. Differentiation is a moral and mathematical obligation. AI labs pay top dollar for human-generated novelty because synthetic data degrades model performance. 59% of sci-fi books about post-scarcity societies are about meaning — not money or status.
items.angle: The connection between Shannon information theory and AI training is literal, not philosophical. Labs pay for unique human experience because training on duplicated outputs produces worse models. Differentiation has actual economic value denominated in AI training data prices.
items.tweetA: Anthropic crossed $44B ARR. Claude Shannon proved information IS surprise — a universe of identical outputs has zero information content. The AI labs are paying top dollar for human-generated novelty precisely because they cannot generate it themselves. Differentiation is not just a moral obligation. It is now priced in.
items.tweetB: 59% of sci-fi books about post-scarcity societies are about the search for meaning. As AI removes scarcity, Frankl's problem surfaces: ever more people have the means to live but no meaning to live for. This is not philosophy — it is asking what happens to consumer demand when productivity is no longer the constraint.

items.title: Project Spire: US Evaluating Israel Negev AI Base as Secure Alternative to Taiwan Semis
items.idea: US and Israel discussing 'Project Spire' — an AI base in Israel's Negev Desert with the security of a US military installation and Silicon Valley creative output. US evaluating 3 Israeli-proposed sites. Long-term lease from Israel. Would include: R&D, large-scale server infrastructure, energy systems, chip fabrication. Explicitly designed to reduce dependence on semiconductor production in 'exposed regions like Taiwan.' Context: Rubio warned embassies that DeepSeek is waging a state-backed campaign to steal US AI technology.
items.angle: The Taiwan hedge nobody is talking about. This is the US government building a contingency AI infrastructure plan for the scenario where Taiwan becomes inaccessible. Two diplomatic tracks running in parallel: negotiate with China while building the fallback.
items.tweetA: US and Israel are discussing Project Spire: an AI base in the Negev with the security of a US military installation. Chip fabrication included — explicitly to reduce Taiwan semiconductor dependence. This is the Taiwan hedge nobody is talking about. If Taiwan becomes inaccessible, the US wants AI infrastructure that cannot be blockaded.
items.tweetB: The Negev AI base would reduce dependence on Taiwan chip production. Context: Trump just landed in Beijing with Jensen Huang. Two tracks running in parallel — negotiate chip access with China while building the contingency for when that negotiation fails. Project Spire is the fallback. Beijing knows it.

items.title: China EV Field Research: $24k Cars, 10-15% Subsidy Stack, Lights-Out Factories Building Ahead
items.idea: China NEV average domestic price ~$24,000 before incentives. Subsidies reduce that another 10-15%. Jiangsu Bojun Industrial (37.4% revenue growth 2025): serves Geely, XPeng, Seres; TRUMPF laser integration; expanding into magnesium alloy die-casting; lights-out automation 24h. Full-cycle integration from mold design through complex assembly. 'They build capacity for expected demand, ensuring they are the only ones ready to ship when the market spikes.' Lower energy prices provide margin of safety to build ahead.
items.angle: The $24k price point with another 10-15% subsidized off the top means the effective consumer price is ~$20k for a full-featured SUV. The competitive arithmetic for Western OEMs is not about labor cost — it is about energy cost structure and willingness to build ahead of demand.
items.tweetA: Chinese NEV average price: ~$24k. Government subsidies reduce that another 10-15%. TRUMPF laser systems in the factory. Magnesium alloy die-casting for lightweighting. 37.4% revenue growth at Jiangsu Bojun in 2025. Western OEMs are competing against a supply chain designed to win on cost AND cycle time simultaneously.
items.tweetB: Western firms wait for guaranteed contracts before building new capacity. Bojun builds ahead of demand. They own the entire value chain from mold design to complex assembly. Lower energy costs provide the margin of safety to take that risk. This is not a labor cost story anymore — it is an energy cost and capital allocation story.

items.title: China Double Reduction Killed $100B Tutoring Industry — Corporate Training Is the Replacement
items.idea: China's 2021 Double Reduction Policy dismantled the $100B+ private K-12 tutoring industry. In its place: government aggressively supporting vocational and corporate training. Shanghai Action Education Technology: 93% of net profit returned as dividends in 2025; high-margin pre-payment model; expanding to 100 campuses by 2030. AI tools: 'Erlang Shen' (talent ID) and 'War God' (marketing). Revenue ¥811M in 2025, net profit ¥320M (+19% y/y).
items.angle: China's regulatory destruction of the tutoring sector redirected demand upward in the value chain. The underlying human desire to invest in education did not disappear — the government redirected it. Template for what regulatory intervention in consumer EdTech looks like at scale.
items.tweetA: China's 2021 Double Reduction Policy destroyed the $100B+ K-12 tutoring industry. In its place: corporate training. Shanghai Action Education returned 93% of net profit as dividends in 2025. They built AI tools called War God and Erlang Shen. Education is where Chinese industrial policy showed up first. The demand doesn't disappear — it gets redirected.
items.tweetB: China EdTech lesson: when regulators ban one vertical, they do not destroy demand — they redirect it. K-12 tutoring banned → corporate/vocational training boomed. The pre-payment model creates a massive guaranteed revenue pool. Western EdTech companies face the same regulatory risk and none of them are prepared for it.

items.source: Neil Sethi / As We Approach the Open
items.title: Morgan Stanley Sets China Equity Targets — 8-12% Upside to Q2 2027
items.idea: Morgan Stanley set new price targets for Q2 2027: Hang Seng 28,400 (+8%), MSCI China 91 (+12%), HSCEI 9,900 (+11%), CSI-300 5,400 (+11%). Bull case: yuan strength vs dollar, improved earnings, upstream supply chain dominance. Strategists led by Laura Wang: 'The sheer size of opportunities in China's equity market at the single stock and thematic level.'
items.angle: The MS China bull case is most at risk from the summit it is benefiting from. If Trump extracts real concessions on Taiwan or tech, Chinese equity upside narrows. If he trades cheap wins, the upside holds. The Morgan Stanley call is a bet on Trump blinking.
items.tweetA: Morgan Stanley sets targets for Q2 2027: Hang Seng +8%, MSCI China +12%, CSI-300 +11%. The bull case: yuan strength, improved earnings, upstream supply chain dominance. The bear case is sitting in the room — Trump is in Beijing right now negotiating the terms of the tech cold war.
items.tweetB: MS says Chinese equities have 8-12% upside over 12 months. The irony: this call is most at risk from the Beijing summit. If Trump extracts real concessions on Taiwan, Chinese upside narrows. If he trades cheap wins, the upside holds. The Morgan Stanley China bull call is a bet that Trump blinks.

items.source: Neil Sethi / As We Approach the Open
items.title: Alibaba First Operating Loss Since COVID — Both Giants Burning Cash to Compete in AI
items.idea: Alibaba posted its first operating loss since COVID depths, underscoring extent of AI spending. Tencent reported slowest revenue growth in 6 quarters as core businesses show signs of aging. SoftBank surged in quarterly profit from OpenAI investment valuation gains.
items.angle: China's tech giants face a triple constraint the US hyperscalers do not: export-controlled chips, domestic AI competitors, and macro headwinds simultaneously. The AI capex cycle has no borders.
items.tweetA: Alibaba posted its first operating loss since COVID. Tencent's slowest growth in 6 quarters. Both burning cash to compete in AI. At the same time, SoftBank surged on OpenAI valuation gains. The AI capex trade is the same everywhere: sacrifice current earnings for future positioning. Who has the balance sheet to wait?
items.tweetB: Alibaba operating loss, Tencent growth stalling — while they face export-controlled chips AND US AI competitors. Contrast with the PRC chip ecosystem, where the IPO market is hot and VC is returning. Infrastructure before applications — the same pattern as the US buildout, just 18 months behind.

items.source: Neil Sethi / As We Approach the Open
items.title: UK Starmer Leadership Crisis — 93 MPs Call for Resignation, Gilts Under Pressure
items.idea: 93 Labour MPs calling for Starmer's resignation; 158 backing him. Health Secretary Wes Streeting preparing to resign and challenge for the leadership. Gilts fell 3-4bps on King's Speech pause but returned under pressure after the 17-minute Starmer-Streeting meeting. King's Speech: closer EU ties, immigration reform, NHS, railways, water. No agreement on Starmer's replacement — his main survival mechanism.
items.angle: UK political instability is a direct gilts risk factor. Every percent of leadership uncertainty means UK borrowing costs need a higher risk premium. The King's Speech legislative agenda is a stabilization tactic, not a resolution.
items.tweetA: 93 Labour MPs calling for Starmer's resignation. Health Secretary Streeting preparing to resign and challenge for leadership. Gilts fell on the King's Speech pause, then immediately tightened on the 17-minute Streeting meeting. UK political risk is back. $GBPUSD watching this closely.
items.tweetB: UK King's Speech: EU ties, immigration, NHS, railways, water. Starmer's legislative agenda is his political survival mechanism. But bond markets don't care about the agenda when the PM might be gone by summer. The gilts market is telling you something the political headlines are not.

items.title: Anthropic Raising $30B at $900B Valuation — Google + SpaceX Planning Orbital Data Centers
items.idea: Anthropic in talks to raise $30B at $900B valuation. Google and SpaceX in talks to launch orbital data centers — taking AI compute infrastructure literally off Earth. xAI quietly added 500MW of unpermitted gas generation at Colossus 2. The infrastructure arms race has exhausted terrestrial solutions: permitting, grid capacity, and water availability are the binding constraints.
items.angle: $900B valuation for Anthropic means the market is pricing AI infrastructure as worth more than Walmart and JPMorgan combined. The space compute story is the logical endpoint of a land-constrained, grid-constrained, water-constrained buildout.
items.tweetA: Anthropic in talks to raise $30B at a $900B valuation. Walmart's market cap is ~$700B. We may be building the most valuable company in human history and calling it a Series F. Separately: Google and SpaceX are planning orbital data centers. The infrastructure arms race has left Earth.
items.tweetB: xAI quietly added 500MW of unpermitted gas at Colossus 2. Anthropic raising at $900B. Google going to orbit for data centers. The AI energy problem is not being solved — it is being outrun. Build faster than the grid can say no. The permitting system is the only constraint left, and Musk just demonstrated you can ignore it.

items.title: 84% of the World Has Never Touched AI — The Biggest Adoption Curve Has Not Started
items.idea: 84% of the world has never touched AI. The entire AI valuation debate, all adoption metrics, productivity claims — all exist within the 16% that is engaged. Global smartphone penetration took 15 years to reach 60%. AI interfaces are already better than early smartphones. Bottleneck: affordability, language support, connectivity in emerging markets.
items.angle: This is the most bullish long-term AI signal available. Every enterprise deployment, every Claude subscription — within 16% of the world. The other 84% is the market yet to be built. The distribution problem, not the technology problem, is the next decade's opportunity.
items.tweetA: 84% of the world has never touched AI. The entire debate about AI valuations exists within the 16% that is engaged. Global smartphones took 15 years to reach 60% penetration. AI is already better than early smartphones. The other 84% is the market yet to be built.
items.tweetB: 84% of the world has never touched AI. Whoever solves the emerging market AI distribution problem — affordability, local language, offline-capable, low-data — wins a market larger than the current US tech sector combined. This is not a 3-year bet. It is a 15-year bet.

items.title: Zebra Technologies +11.4% — Supply Chain Digitization Demand Is Real, Earnings Scale Faster Than Revenue
items.idea: Zebra Technologies Q1: adjusted EPS $4.75 vs $4.25 consensus; revenue $1.49B (+14.3% y/y). Raised FY guidance to $18.30-$18.70 (vs ~$17.7 consensus). The EPS beat ($0.50 above consensus) was much larger than the revenue beat ($0.01B) — operating leverage from cost structure reset during downturn. Demand broadening across retail, logistics, manufacturing for store-level digitization, inventory visibility, workforce productivity, and supply chain modernization.
items.angle: Zebra is the cleanliest leading indicator for enterprise operational technology capex. When retail, logistics, and manufacturing are all simultaneously investing in tracking and automation — that is broad-based enterprise spending confirmation.
items.tweetA: Zebra Technologies: EPS $4.75 vs $4.25 consensus; 14.3% revenue growth; raised FY guidance by $0.60 midpoint. Retail, logistics, manufacturing all investing in supply chain digitization simultaneously. $ZBRA is a tell for enterprise capex health. The AI will replace everything narrative is missing this.
items.tweetB: Zebra's margin story: tiny revenue beat ($0.01B) but massive EPS beat ($0.50). Operating leverage from the cost reset during the downturn — earnings scaling faster than revenue. That is the pattern to look for in any industrial that restructured in 2024. The recovery earnings are outsized. $ZBRA $STE

items.title: Gold Mining M&A Accelerating — Equinox Acquires Orla as Sector Consolidation Deepens
items.idea: Equinox acquiring Orla Mining in the latest wave of gold mining M&A. Accelerating consolidation trend as gold trades above $4,700. Andina Copper continues to hit strong drill results. Sector is using record gold prices and high copper prices to consolidate and build pipeline. M&A wave signals management confidence in sustained precious metals pricing.
items.angle: Gold M&A waves historically signal peak-adjacent confidence. Miners consolidating at $4,700+ gold rather than buying back stock tells you they believe the commodity cycle has more room.
items.tweetA: Equinox acquiring Orla — the gold mining M&A wave is accelerating. Gold at $4,700+, copper at ATH, and miners are using the cash flows to consolidate rather than buy back stock. When miners do M&A at cycle highs, they are telling you they believe there is more to come. $GDX $GDXJ
items.tweetB: Gold mining M&A is the canary: miners consolidate when they have conviction on the commodity outlook. Equinox plus Orla. Andina Copper keeps drilling. At $4,700 gold and copper ATH simultaneously, the miners are as bullish on the commodity cycle as I have seen in years.
